courses.name: Introduction to Feminist Social Transformation
courses.description: Overview of intersectional feminist debates as well as historical and contemporary theoretical traditions in gender and sexuality studies, critical race studies, and disability studies. Topics include the social construction of femininity, masculinity, and other identifications; Indigenous, decolonial, and transnational feminisms.

courses.name: Intersectional Identities
courses.description: Critical examination of the multiple intersections between gender, as a relation of power and social identity, as these intersect with (neo)colonialism, racism, poverty, ableism and heterosexism in a globalized world.
courses.prerequisites: one of WGST 1808 , HRSJ 1001, FYSM 1402 , or permission of the Institute of Women's and Gender Studies.

courses.name: Activism, Feminisms, and Social Justice
courses.description: A comparative, interdisciplinary examination of feminist activism in the modern era.  A range of perspectives and materials are used to examine the objectives, scope,  and impact of feminists' efforts to effect social and political change in different historical, cultural, and national settings.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: Body Matters: The Politics of Bodies
courses.description: Introduction to feminist studies of globalization and politics of bodies. Globalization of ideas, cultures, economics and politics, movement of bodies, bodies as spaces for disrupting norms of sex, gender, race, class, ability, sexuality, embodiment and embodied resistance in a globalized world.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: Sex For Sale
courses.description: Explores feminist perspectives on the sex industry, critically analyzing various legal approaches to regulation and the social meanings assigned to sex work.
courses.prerequisites: Second year standing and WGST 1808 or FYSM 1402 .

courses.name: Masculinities
courses.description: Theoretical, experiential, cultural and policy issues around masculinities studies. The complexities of masculinities; the intersections of feminist and masculinity studies. Topics may include hegemonic, racialized, homosexual, and Other(ed) masculinities. Feminist theories and transnational perspectives frame course content and discussions.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: Selected Topics in Women’s and Gender Studies
courses.description: An interdisciplinary analysis of one or more topics in women’s and gender studies.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: Gender, Sexuality and Cultural Production
courses.description: How gender and cultural (re)production (literature, visual/performing arts, social media) and consumption articulate, circulate, and transform each other within economic, political, and social contexts. Emphasis on role, object, processes, and representations.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: Theory and Research in Feminist Social Transformation
courses.description: Interdisciplinary and intersectional approach introducing students to contemporary feminist, Indigenous, decolonial, and transnational theories, issues, conflicts, methodologies, and critiques of prevailing approaches to the construction of knowledge. Themes include, feminist epistemology, ontology, knowledge, and ethics in feminist research.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing and 1.0 credit in WGST or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Feminisms and Transnationalism
courses.description: Feminist analyses of the diversity of transnational experiences around rights, health, education, motherhood, fathering, work, social media and technological change, among others. Topics may include: migration, environment, wars/conflicts, neocolonialism, diaspora, human trafficking, refugee issues and displaced populations.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing, and 1.0 credit in WGST; or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Girlhoods
courses.description: The emerging discipline of girlhood studies; social and cultural constructions of girlhood and categories of difference. Topics may include the commercialization of girlhood, popular culture and girls, negotiating identities, violence, sexualities, agency and activism in a globalizing world.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing and 1.0 credit in WGST or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Gendered Violence
courses.description: Theories, concepts and contexts of the complex manifestations of gendered violence in the lives of women, men and children globally.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing and 1.0 credit in WGST or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Selected Topics in Women's and Gender Studies
courses.description: An interdisciplinary analysis of one or more topics in women's and gender studies.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing and 1.0 credit in WGST.

courses.name: Co-operative Work Term
courses.description: This course covers the deliverables associated with the co-op work term, such as the site visit, work term report submission, and employer evaluation.
courses.prerequisites: Registration in the Co-operative Education Option.

courses.name: Traversing Feminisms
courses.description: Interdisciplinary overview of key historical concepts and issues in Women’s and Gender Studies in areas of theory, epistemology, and research design. This course is designed for students pursuing research/studies in WGST, DBST, CRST, and/or SXST who have not taken  any courses in our institute.
courses.prerequisites: permission of the Institute.

courses.name: African Feminisms
courses.description: African feminisms as theoretical interventions and as political practice, and as diverse forms. Gender as a marker of power: status, hierarchy, social capability, and as a system of distribution of resources, responsibilities and solidarities.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth year standing and WGST 1808 or FYSM 1402 OR permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Transgender Human Rights
courses.description: Critical analyses of human rights through an examination of transgender subjectivities. The systemic erasure of trans people within society and the struggles of some activists to normalize trans identities.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing.

courses.name: Women’s and Gender Studies Practicum
courses.description: Experience in research through a combination of classroom seminars and a field placement. Each project will be negotiated individually as a contract between the student, instructor and institutional partner.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth year standing and WGST 3001 with a minimum 6.5 CGPA in B.A. Hons. Women’s and Gender Studies program or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Women's and Gender Studies Practicum
courses.description: Experience in applied feminisms through a combination of classroom seminars and internship. Each project will be negotiated individually as a contract between the student, instructor and institutional partner. Students must complete both the in-class and the internship portion of the course.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth year standing and WGST 3001 with a minimum 6.5 CGPA in B.A. Hons. Women's and Gender Studies program or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Honours Research Project in Women’s and Gender Studies
courses.description: Students will undertake a major research project on some aspect of women’s and gender studies under the supervision of a faculty member.
courses.prerequisites: A major CGPA of at least 11.00, plus WGST 3809 and WGST 3810 OR WGST 3001 and fourth-year standing in B.A. Hons. Women’s and Gender Studies program, or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Selected Topics in Women’s and Gender Studies
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing and 1.0 credit in Women’s and Gender Studies or permission of the  Institute of Women’s and Gender Studies.

courses.name: Independent Study
courses.description: Reading or research course supervised by a faculty member. Written proposal approved by the supervisor must be submitted before last day of course changes. Normally, only 0.5 credit of independent study may be counted in the program.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in a Women’s and Gender Studies program or permission of the Institute.

free_electives.degree: Women's and Gender Studies B.A. Honours (20.0 credits)
Women's and Gender Studies B.A. (15.0 credits)
Specialization in Global Genders and Sexualities B.G.In.S. Honours (20.0 credits)
Stream in Global Genders and Sexualities B.G.In.S. (15.0 credits)
